Package io.cloudstub.dynamodb

Class CloudStubDynamoDBApiService

java.lang.Object
io.cloudstub.dynamodb.CloudStubDynamoDBApiService
All Implemented Interfaces:
CloudStubApiService
public class CloudStubDynamoDBApiService extends Object implements CloudStubApiService
REST API surface for DynamoDB, mounted under /api/dynamodb/….

Each route advertises a CLI command name and its parameters via /api/status, so the CLI can build clb dynamodb <command> dynamically with no compile-time knowledge of DynamoDB.

Routes are state-backed: they read the same StateStore (under the same DynamoKeys scheme) as the AWS-protocol stubs in CloudStubDynamoDBService, so an item put through the AWS SDK is listed by GET /api/dynamodb/scan and shown in the console. The surface is read-oriented — items are typed attribute maps that do not map cleanly to query-string parameters — so writes go through the AWS SDK.

Discovered via META-INF/services/io.cloudstub.core.spi.CloudStubApiService.

    • registerRoutes

      public void registerRoutes(CloudStubApiContext context)
      Description copied from interface: CloudStubApiService
      Called at API server startup. Register all module-specific routes through context.registrar(); routes are mounted at /api/<serviceId><path>. The same context.stateStore() the module's stubs use is available here, so REST routes can return live data instead of synthetic stubs.
      Specified by:
      registerRoutes in interface CloudStubApiService
